decks.notes: The primary strategy of this deck is to control the game through counterspells and removal, while also utilizing the powerful combo of Oath of Druids and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to cheat out powerful creatures onto the battlefield. The deck includes various mana acceleration cards such as Black Lotus and Moxen to quickly ramp into its threats. It also has versatile cards like Show and Tell and Tinker to further disrupt the opponent's plans and win the game through its powerful creatures like Oko, Thief of Crowns and Bolas's Citadel.

This deck focuses on generating card advantage and mana through artifacts like Moxen and Mana Crypt, and using cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain to create a powerful combo. The deck aims to quickly assemble key pieces like Time Vault and Bolas's Citadel, while disrupting opponents with Force of Will and other counter spells. By utilizing powerful artifacts and combo pieces, the deck seeks to overwhelm opponents with a powerful and synergistic strategy that can win the game in just a few turns.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of countermagic, removal spells, and taxing effects such as Lavinia, Azorius Renegade. It also utilizes powerful card draw spells like Dig Through Time and Ancestral Recall to maintain card advantage. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value by recurring low-cost permanents. Overall, the deck aims to control the board state and outvalue the opponent in a Vintage 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade. It also utilizes efficient draw spells like Brainstorm and Dig Through Time to maintain card advantage. The deck includes powerful restricted cards like Ancestral Recall and Time Walk, as well as mana acceleration from Black Lotus and Moxen. Additionally, the inclusion of a companion, Lurrus of the Dream-Den, adds another layer of consistency to the overall strategy. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually building towards a win condition.

The key strategy of this Doomsday Combo deck is to quickly and efficiently assemble the necessary combo pieces to win the game. Through efficient card draw, tutoring, and powerful spells like Doomsday and Thassa's Oracle, the deck aims to set up a winning combination of cards in a single turn. With a mix of control elements like Force of Will and disruption like Duress, the deck can protect its combo and disrupt opponents' strategies. The deck's key strength lies in its ability to win the game in a single explosive turn, catching opponents off guard.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Dredge mechanic to quickly fill the graveyard with cards like Golgari Thug, Stinkweed Imp, and Golgari Grave-Troll, enabling powerful combos with cards like Narcomoeba and Prized Amalgam. The deck also includes disruption spells like Force of Will and Mindbreak Trap to protect the combo. Bazaar of Baghdad is key to drawing cards and fueling the graveyard. Creeping Chill provides additional reach and life gain. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence and graveyard synergies.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 Aggro-MUD deck focuses on disrupting opponents with artifacts like Chalice of the Void, Sphere of Resistance, and Trinisphere while also utilizing mana acceleration from Mishra's Workshop and mana-producing artifacts like the Mox series. The deck aims to quickly deploy threats such as Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Lodestone Golem to overpower opponents before they can stabilize. Crucible of Worlds and Inventors' Fair provide additional utility, while Nettlecyst and Patchwork Automaton offer flexible win conditions. The deck's efficient usage of mana resources and disruptive elements create a powerful aggressive strategy.

The deck focuses on using Paradoxical Outcome to generate card advantage and mana to combo off with powerful spells like Time Walk and Ancestral Recall. It includes cards that provide incremental value such as Thundertrap Trainer and Hexing Squelcher, while also running disruptive spells like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect the combo. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion gives the deck additional flexibility and resilience. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently to overwhelm the opponent and win the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful artifacts and land destruction to disrupt the opponent's strategy while deploying aggressive creatures to quickly overwhelm them. It includes key pieces like Null Rod, Thorn of Amethyst, and Crucible of Worlds to control the game's tempo while employing cards like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Mystic Forge to generate additional resources and card advantage. The inclusion of Moxen, Wasteland, and Ancient Tomb allow for explosive starts and mana acceleration, setting up the aggressive MUD archetype to dominate the battlefield and close out the game swiftly.

The deck focuses on using Doomsday to manipulate the top of the library and combo off with Thassa's Oracle to win the game. It includes multiple cantrip and draw spells to dig for combo pieces, along with interaction such as Force of Will and Spell Pierce to protect the combo. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional versatility and recursion. The deck is built to quickly assemble the combo, disrupt the opponent's game plan, and win in a concise and efficient manner.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to ramp quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disruptive spells. The key strategy involves using cards like Mishra's Workshop, Grim Monolith, and Moxen to generate large amounts of mana, while deploying threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Scrawling Crawler to apply pressure. Cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void help control the opponent's plays, while Tezzeret, the Great Creator and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional utility and versatility. Overall, the deck aims to win through a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive artifacts.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, card draw, and tutors to find key combo pieces like Gifts Ungiven, Underworld Breach, and Brain Freeze to mill the opponent in a single turn. It also utilizes powerful mana acceleration such as Moxen and mana rocks to quickly cast spells and generate value. The companion Lurrus of the Dream-Den provides additional utility and recursion for key cards. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win condition through a combination of card advantage and combo potential.

The deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive artifact creatures like Stonecoil Serpent and Patchwork Automaton, backed up by disruptive artifacts like Trinisphere and Null Rod to slow down the opponent's game plan. The deck also utilizes Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb for quick mana acceleration, while using cards like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Karn, the Great Creator to search for key artifacts from the sideboard to further enhance its game plan. The deck aims to control the board with a combination of powerful creatures and disruptive artifacts to secure a quick victory.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while generating card advantage through powerful cards like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time. It utilizes counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce to control the board and removal spells like Fatal Push to deal with threats efficiently. Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as a powerful companion providing additional value by recurring cheap permanents from the graveyard. Overall, the deck aims to outlast opponents, grind out advantage, and eventually win through efficient resource management and control.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with cards like Stinkweed Imp and Golgari Thug to enable powerful graveyard-based combos. Cards like Creeping Chill and Prized Amalgam provide additional value from the graveyard, while cards like Force of Will and Force of Negation help protect the combo pieces. Bazaar of Baghdad helps to set up the graveyard quickly, while Hollow One and Ichorid provide additional threats to pressure the opponent. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient graveyard strategy.

The deck focuses on using Paradoxical Outcome to generate card advantage and combo off by casting multiple spells in a single turn to overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes powerful mana rocks like Black Lotus and Moxen to generate mana quickly, along with powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Gitaxian Probe. The deck also includes key combo pieces like Time Vault and Manifold Key to create an infinite turn loop. Force of Will and other interaction help protect the combo while Lurrus of the Dream-Den provides additional value and recursion.
